messages.message: You can create localized strings to have strings change depending on the locale. This allows to easely change the language by changing the locale.

When concatenating something to a localized string, the resulting string will never be localized. This is especially important when using the += operator since it will make the localized string literal.

One way to solve the concatenation problem is to refresh the strings when the locale is changed. This can be done by overriding the refresh() method of any widget.

${block:Warning-message "You should always call the refresh() method of the parent class when overriding refresh(). Otherwise, the widget may not be refreshed correctly."}

Another way to solve the concatenation problem is to use WString formatting mechanisms. This allows to replace placeholders in a WSring with other strings or WStrings.

${block:Warning-message "A literal WSring will not be updated when the locale is changed, even if a localized WString replaces one of its placeholder."} ${block:Warning-message "You should keep in mind that formating a WString will not create a new WString, but modify the existing one."}
